message:
fix(doctor): ARM64 Linux meta_skill detection + update Bun deps

- doctor.sh: detect aarch64-Linux and show helpful warning instead of 404
- doctor.sh: case statement for platform-specific install guidance
- Update @types/node 22.19.3 → 22.19.11
- Regenerate manifest (SHA256 verified, no drift)
- Close bd-5mes9 (deps update), add Part 1 note to bd-eh383

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -993,9 +993,9 @@
 {"id":"bd-3y1n","title":"EPIC: No-Brainer ACFS Improvements (Jan 2026)","description":"## Epic: No-Brainer ACFS Improvements (Jan 2026)

This epic contains 15 high-impact, low-complexity improvements to the ACFS project that will obviously benefit users with minimal risk. Each improvement was evaluated against these criteria:

**Evaluation Criteria:**
- Robust: Does it make the system more resilient?
- Reliable: Does it reduce failure modes?
- Performant: Does it make things faster?
- Intuitive: Is it discoverable and obvious?
- User-friendly: Does it reduce friction?
- Ergonomic: Does it fit natural workflows?
- Useful: Does it solve real problems?
- Compelling: Will users be glad it exists?
- Accretive: Does it build on existing strengths?
- Pragmatic: Is the effort justified by the benefit?

## Top 5 (Highest Impact)
1. **bd-3nbx**: `acfs doctor --fix` - Auto-remediation mode
2. **bd-2dkb**: Copy-to-clipboard for web wizard code blocks
3. **bd-29fl**: `acfs status` - One-line health summary
4. **bd-zhdi**: Shell tab completion for acfs command
5. **bd-2gys**: Step validation in web wizard

## Next 10 (High Value, Complementary)
6. **bd-21kh**: Progress bar during tool installation
7. **bd-39ye**: NO_COLOR environment variable support
8. **bd-1lug**: Rate limit backoff for GitHub API calls
9. **bd-1eop**: Skip already-installed tools during install
10. **bd-w8fx**: VPS provider comparison table
11. **bd-1yfv**: "I'm stuck" help button in web wizard
12. **bd-2p56**: `acfs changelog` command
13. **bd-331g**: Dark mode toggle in web wizard
14. **bd-2zqr**: Webhook notification on install completion
15. **bd-3jd9**: `acfs export-config` command

## Implementation Notes
- Each bead is self-contained with full implementation details
- Start with top 5 for maximum early impact
- Beads 6-10 are quick wins that complement the top 5
- Beads 11-15 are nice-to-haves that round out the experience

## Success Criteria
- All 15 beads implemented and tested
- No regressions in existing functionality
- Documentation updated for new features
- User feedback collected on improvements","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:00:18.624398983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:33:42.559881815Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:33:39.119946333Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"bd-3y1n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closing EPIC to unblock 13 child tasks. bd-2dkb (copy-to-clipboard) completed. Remaining children are now actionable for individual implementation.","created_at":"2026-01-26T22:33:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-47sq","title":"Deep exploration: MS (Meta Skill)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of MS (Meta Skill) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify MS installation
[[ -d /dp/meta_skill ]] && echo "PASS: ms repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: ms repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v ms &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: ms command available" || { echo "FAIL: ms not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check Claude Code skill integration
[[ -d ~/.claude/skills ]] && echo "INFO: Claude skills directory exists" || echo "INFO: No skills directory yet"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/ms-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/meta_skill/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for skill authoring docs, SKILL.md format

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Skill creation workflow
  - SKILL.md format and requirements
  - Skill validation mechanism
  - Installation into Claude Code
  - Skill discovery and loading

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
ms --help 2>&1 | head -20
ms create --help 2>&1 | head -10
ms validate --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
ms list 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'meta skill OR ms OR skill authoring'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'ms skill create' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/meta_skill/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/meta_skill && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Skill creation workflow
- [ ] SKILL.md format specification
- [ ] Validation rules and errors
- [ ] Installation process
- [ ] Skill discovery mechanism

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] Claude Code - primary integration target
- [ ] sc (skill) - skill creation helper
- [ ] sw (skill writer) - advanced skill authoring

### 2.3 Format Verification
```bash
# Check SKILL.md template
cat /dp/meta_skill/templates/SKILL.md 2>/dev/null | head -30
# Or find example skills
find /dp -name "SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | head -5 | xargs head -20
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update ms entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Skill authoring for Claude Code
- `description`: Creation and validation
- `deepDescription`: How skill system works
- `features`: Verified capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test ms entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const ms = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'ms');
console.log('Testing ms entry...');
console.assert(ms, 'ms entry exists');
console.assert(ms.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(ms.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Skill Operations
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/ms-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== MS E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: List skills
echo "Test 1: List skills..." | tee -a $LOG
ms list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Validate a skill
echo "Test 2: Validate..." | tee -a $LOG
ms validate /dp/meta_skill 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "Validation done"

# Test 3: Create help
echo "Test 3: Create help..." | tee -a $LOG
ms create --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-47sq --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Skill format documented correctly
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:36.485651577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:05.943130894Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:05.943105255Z","close_reason":"MS deep exploration complete: verified v0.1.0, 60+ commands, 12 MCP tools (corrected from 6). Updated flywheel.ts and tldr-content.ts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-53zvp","title":"Test: verify claude update --channel latest works correctly","description":"## What
Verify that the --channel latest fix actually works end-to-end.

## Test Plan

### Pre-flight check
1. Run `claude --version` to record current version and channel
2. Run `claude update --help` to confirm --channel flag exists

### Test 1: Dry-run validation of update.sh
1. Run `bash scripts/lib/update.sh --dry-run --verbose 2>&1 | grep -i "claude update"` 
2. Verify: ALL logged claude update commands include "--channel latest"
3. Verify: No bare "claude update" appears in the dry-run output
4. Log: All claude update commands found in dry-run output

### Test 2: uca alias validation
1. Source acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc
2. Run `alias uca` or `type uca`
3. Verify: alias contains "--channel latest"
4. Log: Full alias definition

### Test 3: Live update test (if safe)
1. Run `claude update --channel latest 2>&1`
2. Verify: exit code 0
3. Verify: version output shows "latest" channel or version >= current
4. Log: Full update output, before/after versions

### Test 4: Grep-based completeness check
1. Run `grep -n "claude update" scripts/lib/update.sh | grep -v "channel"` 
2. Verify: NO matches (every occurrence has --channel)
3. Also check: `grep -n "claude update" acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc | grep -v "channel"`
4. Verify: NO matches
5. Log: Any remaining bare "claude update" occurrences

## Output Format
```
[TEST_1] PASS: All dry-run claude update commands include --channel latest (5/5)
[TEST_2] PASS: uca alias = "~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest"
[TEST_3] PASS: Live update succeeded, channel=latest version=2.1.37
[TEST_4] PASS: No bare "claude update" found in update.sh or acfs.zshrc
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 tests pass
- Zero bare "claude update" anywhere in the codebase","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:35:47.930709260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:41:41.035926441Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:41:41.035906184Z","close_reason":"Superseded by bd-gsjqf.4. Original test plan was invalid (--channel flag doesn't exist). Comprehensive tests in bd-gsjqf.4 cover the actual verified installer approach.","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#125/test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude-code","testing","update"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-53zvp","depends_on_id":"bd-gsjqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:35:52.695521713Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-53zvp","depends_on_id":"bd-gsjqf.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:29:24.109266113Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":60,"issue_id":"bd-53zvp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TEST PLAN INVALIDATED: Tests 1-4 all assume the fix is claude update --channel latest. Since --channel does NOT exist as a CLI flag (verified by running claude update --help), the test criteria must change. If the fix switches to update_run_verified_installer, Test 1 should grep for update_run_verified_installer calls, Test 2 should check the uca alias for the new approach, Test 3 should verify the installer works, and Test 4 should grep for bare claude update occurrences. The specific test criteria depend on which fix approach is chosen (see bd-gsjqf comments for alternatives).","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:18:48Z"},{"id":61,"issue_id":"bd-53zvp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SUPERSEDED: This test plan is invalid because `claude update --channel latest` does not exist as a CLI flag (verified by running `claude update --help`). All 4 tests in this bead assume --channel latest works. Replaced by bd-gsjqf.4 which tests the actual fix approach (verified installer). This bead should be closed when bd-gsjqf.4 is completed.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:29:22Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-5mes9","title":"Update all dependencies to latest stable versions","description":"## Dependency Update for agentic_coding_flywheel_setup

### Context
ACFS is primarily Bash scripts + a Bun/TypeScript manifest generator. Dependencies are:
- Bun packages in `packages/manifest/package.json`
- System tools installed by the setup scripts
- Shell libraries in `scripts/lib/`

### Steps
1. `cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup`
2. Update Bun packages: `cd packages/manifest && bun update && cd ../..`
3. Regenerate manifest index: `cd packages/manifest && bun run generate && cd ../..`
4. Verify manifest SHA256 matches: `scripts/check-manifest-drift.sh --json`
5. Test installer: run a dry-run or check syntax with `bash -n install.sh`
6. Verify acfs CLI works: `source acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc && acfs doctor`

### Key Dependencies to Watch
- `@types/node` -- TypeScript definitions
- `yaml` -- YAML parsing in manifest generator
- `zod` -- schema validation (if used)
- System tools: claude, codex, gemini CLI tools

### Verification
- Manifest regeneration produces valid output
- `install.sh` syntax check passes
- `acfs doctor` passes all checks
- SHA256 hash in manifest_index.sh matches acfs.manifest.yaml","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:19:53.136401139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:44:34.542169004Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

2. Proper categorization as utilities
3. -{"id":"bd-eh383","title":"meta_skill ARM64 Linux binary missing — blocked upstream (GH #115)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #115 filed by tayiorbeii (2026-02-06). On ARM64 Linux (aarch64), meta_skill install 404s because v0.1.0 has no aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu binary.

## Available binaries in v0.1.0:
- ms-0.1.0-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz (ARM64 macOS - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz (x86_64 Linux - exists)
- ms-0.1.0-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip (Windows - exists)
- aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu — MISSING

## Two-Part Fix

### Part 1: ACFS workaround (THIS repo, can do now)
Update doctor.sh check at lines 1115-1116 to detect aarch64 on Linux and show:
  "meta_skill: ARM64 Linux binary not yet available (see https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill/issues/1)"
instead of the generic install suggestion that will 404.

Implementation:
```bash
# In doctor.sh, before the meta_skill install suggestion:
if [[ "$(uname -m)" == "aarch64" ]] && [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Linux" ]]; then
    log_item "warn" "meta_skill (ms)" "ARM64 Linux binary not yet available"
    return 0  # Skip install attempt
fi
```

### Part 2: Upstream (meta_skill repo, blocked)
Add aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu to the GoReleaser or release CI matrix in Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill. Requires:
- Cross-compilation target or dedicated ARM64 runner
- New release (v0.1.1 or v0.2.0)
This is blocked on upstream release work.

## Testing
After Part 1:
1. On this VPS (x86_64): `acfs doctor` should still show meta_skill normally
2. Simulate ARM64: mock uname output or grep the code to verify the aarch64 check exists
3. Verify the issue URL is correct and accessible

## Key Files
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh (lines 1109-1117, meta_skill check)
- Upstream: Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill release CI matrix

## Status
Part 1 is actionable now. Part 2 is blocked upstream. Keep this bead open until both parts are done.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:39.015874050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:31.599422934Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#115","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arm64","bug","meta_skill","upstream-blocked"]}

 {"id":"bd-flfe","title":"Deep exploration: ACFS (Agentic Coding Flywheel Setup)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of ACFS (Agentic Coding Flywheel Setup) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify ACFS installation
[[ -d /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup ]] && echo "PASS: acfs repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: acfs repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v acfs &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: acfs command available" || { echo "FAIL: acfs not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check for manifest
[[ -f /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml ]] && echo "INFO: Manifest exists" || echo "WARN: No manifest"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/acfs-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/README.md` - Read full README
- `cat /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml` - Check manifest structure

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Tool installation mechanism
  - Checksum verification (checksums.yaml)
  - Manifest structure and tool definitions
  - Dependency ordering
  - AGENTS.md generation

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
acfs --help 2>&1 | head -20
acfs install --help 2>&1 | head -10
acfs verify --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
acfs status 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 Security Verification
```bash
# Check checksums file
cat /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/checksums.yaml | head -20

# Verify checksum mechanism
acfs verify --checksums 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No verify command"
```

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Tool installation mechanism
- [ ] Checksum verification process
- [ ] Manifest structure (tools, dependencies)
- [ ] AGENTS.md generation
- [ ] Environment setup

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
ACFS is the bootstrap tool - verify it installs these:
- [ ] All flywheel tools (ntm, mail, br, etc.)
- [ ] Dependencies (rust, bun, uv, etc.)
- [ ] Skills and integrations

### 2.3 Manifest Verification
```bash
# Count tools in manifest
grep -c "^  [a-z]" /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo "Count manually"

# Check dependencies
grep -A5 "depends_on" /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/acfs.manifest.yaml | head -20
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update acfs entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Bootstrap installer for flywheel
- `description`: Tool installation and verification
- `deepDescription`: How manifest-based installation works
- `features`: Verified capabilities (checksums, AGENTS.md)
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: All tools it installs

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Bootstrap workflow

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test acfs entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const acfs = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'acfs');
console.log('Testing acfs entry...');
console.assert(acfs, 'acfs entry exists');
console.assert(acfs.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(acfs.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Installation Verification
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/acfs-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== ACFS E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Status
echo "Test 1: Status..." | tee -a $LOG
acfs status 2>&1 | head -15 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Verify checksums
echo "Test 2: Verify..." | tee -a $LOG
acfs verify 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No verify command"

# Test 3: List tools
echo "Test 3: List..." | tee -a $LOG
acfs list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No list command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-flfe --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Manifest structure documented correctly
- [ ] Checksum mechanism documented
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:02:56.720155271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:35:19.069946681Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:35:19.069916263Z","close_reason":"ACFS deep exploration complete: verified v0.5.0, acfs doctor (47+ checks), acfs update (category-specific), acfs cheatsheet (50+ aliases), acfs session.  {"id":"bd-gsjqf","title":"Fix update.sh Claude Code channel: bare 'claude update' downgrades to stable (GH #125)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #125 filed by joyshmitz (2026-02-08). The install path was fixed (commit 4d0174f0 — agents.sh now uses @latest), but update.sh still calls bare "claude update" which forces the stable channel.

## Problem
run_cmd_claude_update() in scripts/lib/update.sh (line 1230) calls bare "claude update" at lines 1254, 1260, 1267, 1269, 1274. This always uses the stable channel. On a SUCCESSFUL update, users get silently downgraded from latest (e.g., 2.1.37) to stable (e.g., 2.1.25). The fallback path (update_run_verified_installer claude latest at line 1157) correctly uses latest, but only fires when "claude update" FAILS.

Additionally, the uca alias in acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc line 158 uses bare "~/.local/bin/claude update" (also stable channel). The full alias chains claude update with codex and gemini updates: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'

## CRITICAL: --channel flag DOES NOT EXIST
Verified by running: claude update --help => only -h/--help available. The originally proposed fix (claude update --channel latest) is INVALID. This was confirmed by running the actual command on this VPS.

## Chosen Fix: Replace run_cmd_claude_update() with verified installer
The update_run_verified_installer function already exists at update.sh:715-737 and correctly handles the latest channel. It:
1. Looks up the installer URL from KNOWN_INSTALLERS[claude] = "https://claude.ai/install.sh" (security.sh:183)
2. Fetches the installer script
3. Verifies its SHA-256 checksum against checksums.yaml
4. Pipes to bash with args (e.g., "latest")
The fallback path at line 1157 already uses this: run_cmd "Claude Code (reinstall)" update_run_verified_installer claude latest

The fix replaces the entire run_cmd_claude_update() function body with a call to update_run_verified_installer, preserving the logging/error-handling wrapper while switching the underlying mechanism from bare "claude update" to the verified installer with the "latest" argument.

For the uca alias, replace the "~/.local/bin/claude update" portion with an inline verified-installer invocation, OR redirect through a wrapper script, OR call the update function. The alias must preserve the codex and gemini update chain.

## Affected Files
- scripts/lib/update.sh (lines 1229-1296, run_cmd_claude_update(); lines 1150-1161, caller)
- acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc (line 158, uca alias)
- scripts/lib/security.sh (line 183, KNOWN_INSTALLERS[claude] — reference only)
- scripts/lib/agents.sh (line 21, CLAUDE_PACKAGE — reference only; line 148, verified installer call — reference for pattern)

## Sub-tasks (see child beads)
1. Audit: scan for ALL bare "claude update" occurrences (bd-gsjqf.1)
2. Rewrite run_cmd_claude_update() to use verified installer (bd-gsjqf.2)
3. Fix uca alias and any other occurrences found in audit (bd-gsjqf.3)
4. Comprehensive testing (bd-gsjqf.4)
5. End-to-end verification and GH #125 close (bd-gsjqf.5)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:16:19.008573344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:41:34.439322663Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:41:34.439303427Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete (.1-.5). Replaced bare 'claude update' with verified installer (update_run_verified_installer claude latest) in update.sh and uca alias. 9/9 tests pass, live system on latest channel (2.1.39). GH#125 closed.","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup#125","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","claude-code","update"],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPORTANT: Before implementing, verify that 'claude update --channel latest' is a valid CLI flag. Run 'claude update --help' to check. If --channel does not exist, the fallback approach is: replace the native claude update call with update_run_verified_installer() using 'latest' as the primary path (this function already exists in update.sh and correctly handles the latest channel). Also search for any other aliases that might call bare 'claude update' besides the uca alias (e.g., grep for 'claude update' across all acfs shell configs).","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:59:20Z"},{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BUG IN PROPOSED FIX: The uca alias change oversimplifies. Bead says change alias uca from just claude update to claude update --channel latest. But the ACTUAL alias at line 158 is: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'. Following the bead instructions literally would DESTROY the codex and gemini update steps. CORRECT FIX: Change only the claude update portion to claude update --channel latest, preserving the full chain: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:41Z"},{"id":58,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CRITICAL: --channel flag DOES NOT EXIST. Running 'claude update --help' shows only -h/--help as options. The proposed fix (claude update --channel latest) will either fail with unknown option or be silently ignored. VERIFIED BY RUNNING: claude update --help => 'Usage: claude update|upgrade [options] Options: -h, --help Display help for command'. THE FALLBACK APPROACH FROM COMMENT 1 IS NOW THE PRIMARY FIX: Replace all 5 bare 'claude update' calls in run_cmd_claude_update() with update_run_verified_installer claude latest (which already exists in update.sh and correctly handles the latest channel at line 1157). For the uca alias, replace '~/.local/bin/claude update' with a function call or inline the verified installer logic. This is a larger change than originally scoped — the function run_cmd_claude_update() essentially becomes a wrapper around update_run_verified_installer. ALTERNATIVE: Check if claude stores channel info in ~/.claude/ config files and whether the update mechanism reads it. If so, we could set the channel config once rather than changing every update call.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:18:19Z"},{"id":59,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FOLLOW-UP: The round 4 uca alias fix (comment 2) also used --channel latest which does not exist. The corrected alias suggestion (preserving codex/gemini chain) is also wrong in its claude update portion. The uca alias fix depends on the chosen approach for run_cmd_claude_update. If we switch to update_run_verified_installer, the alias would need to call a function or script that wraps update_run_verified_installer, since aliases cannot easily call bash functions defined in update.sh. Alternatively, the uca alias could be changed to invoke the acfs-update command directly (e.g., 'acfs-update --only=claude') if such a flag exists, or to call the official installer inline: alias uca='curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | sh -s -- --channel latest && ...' (need to verify the exact installer URL and flags).","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:18:40Z"},{"id":69,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW FIX — Version/Channel Verification Method: `claude --version` outputs "2.1.37 (Claude Code)" — it does NOT show the channel name. To verify channel programmatically, use:
```
npm view @anthropic-ai/claude-code dist-tags
=> { stable: "2.1.25", latest: "2.1.37", next: "2.1.37" }
```
Compare installed version against dist-tags. If installed matches `latest` dist-tag, we are on the latest channel. If it matches `stable` dist-tag, we got downgraded. This verification method is used in bd-gsjqf.4 (Test 8) and bd-gsjqf.5 (E2E Steps 1, 5, 7).","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:39:22Z"},{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"HISTORICAL NOTE for future readers: Comments 1-4 on this epic document the DISCOVERY PROCESS of the bug. Comments 1 and 2 suggested --channel latest as a fix. Comment 3 discovered --channel does not exist. Comment 4 explored alternatives. The FINAL APPROACH is documented in the epic description body and in the sub-task beads (.1-.5). When implementing, IGNORE comments 1-2 fix suggestions and follow the sub-task descriptions instead.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:40:26Z"},{"id":76,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CONFIRMED: install.sh DOES accept "latest" as $1. Downloaded and inspected https://claude.ai/install.sh:
- Line 6: TARGET="$1"
- Line 9-10: Validates TARGET against ^(stable|latest|VERSION_REGEX)$
- Line 98-99: Always downloads latest binary from GCS bucket
- Line 142: "$binary_path" install ${TARGET:+"$TARGET"} — passes TARGET to binary self-install
So `bash -s -- latest` correctly installs the latest-channel version. The install.sh also has its OWN checksum verification (downloads manifest.json, verifies binary hash). This means even the uca alias Option A (curl|bash) gets checksumming from install.sh itself, PLUS ACFS adds an outer SHA-256 check on install.sh via security.sh. Two layers of verification.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:04:17Z"},{"id":82,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FRESH-EYES REVIEW COMPLETE (2026-02-09). Summary of findings applied to sub-tasks:

bd-gsjqf.1: Added 9 missing documentation/comment occurrences (total scope: 15 items, not 6)
bd-gsjqf.2: (1) INTENTIONAL marker needed on security fallback line for Test 7 compat, (2) lines 2151/2187 need to be in this bead's scope, (3) VERBOSE streaming note, (4) \!= display artifact note
bd-gsjqf.3: (1) Explicit 7-file documentation list added, (2) CRITICAL: must run manifest generate after acfs.manifest.yaml edit, (3) Final uca alias with pipefail confirmed
bd-gsjqf.4: (1) CRITICAL BUG: ((PASS++)) crashes under set -e when PASS=0 — must use PASS=$((PASS + 1)), (2) Test 7 needs INTENTIONAL exclusion, (3) Confirmed test stubbing works without extras
bd-gsjqf.5: (1) Step 6 requires zsh shell, (2) Add manifest SHA256 verification step

All findings are documented as comments on the respective sub-tasks. Dependency graph: .1 -> .2 -> .3 -> .4 -> .5 (with .3 also depending on .1). No cycles. bv diagnostics clean.

 {"id":"bd-gsjqf.1","title":"Audit: scan all files for bare "claude update" occurrences","description":"## What
Scan the entire ACFS codebase for every occurrence of bare "claude update" (without --channel or equivalent). This determines the full scope of the fix before any code changes.

## Why (P1 — scope discovery)
We know about 5 occurrences in update.sh and 1 in acfs.zshrc, but there could be others in documentation, other shell configs, helper scripts, or CI workflows. Missing even one occurrence means users can still get silently downgraded.

## Scan Commands
```bash
# Primary search: all shell scripts
grep -rn "claude update" scripts/ acfs/ --include="*.sh" --include="*.zsh" --include="*.zshrc" --include="*.bashrc"

# Broader search: all files (might find docs, CI, configs)
grep -rn "claude update" . --exclude-dir=.git --exclude-dir=node_modules --exclude-dir=.beads

# Specifically check for the alias pattern
grep -rn "uca\|claude update" acfs/
```

## Expected Findings (from prior analysis)
1. scripts/lib/update.sh:1254 — `claude update 2>&1 | tee -a ...`
2. scripts/lib/update.sh:1260 — `claude update >> ...`
3. scripts/lib/update.sh:1267 — `claude update || exit_code=$?`
4. scripts/lib/update.sh:1269 — `claude update >/dev/null 2>&1 || ...`
5. scripts/lib/update.sh:1274 — `output=$(claude update 2>&1) || ...`
6. acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc:158 — uca alias with `~/.local/bin/claude update`

## Deliverable
A comment on this bead listing EVERY occurrence with file:line, plus whether each is:
- (a) bare "claude update" that needs fixing
- (b) already using verified installer (reference only)
- (c) documentation/comment (may need updating)
- (d) test/CI (may need updating)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero occurrences of bare "claude update" remain unaccounted for
- Each finding classified as (a)/(b)/(c)/(d)
- Full scope documented for bd-gsjqf.2 and bd-gsjqf.3 to consume","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:25:03.950395729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:19:10.295561371Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:19:10.295538899Z","close_reason":"Audit complete: found 9 occurrences needing fix across update.sh (6 exec + 2 doc), acfs.zshrc (1 alias), and acfs.manifest.yaml (1 doc). Security fallback at ~line 1207 already correct.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","claude-code","update"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf.1","depends_on_id":"bd-gsjqf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:25:03.950395729Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":62,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BACKGROUND: This audit is the first step because we discovered in review round 6 that the scope was larger than initially thought. We knew about 5 occurrences in run_cmd_claude_update() and 1 in the uca alias, but there may be more in docs, CI, other shell configs, or helper scripts. The audit prevents us from fixing 6/N occurrences and thinking we are done. PRIORITY: Run this BEFORE any code changes so the scope document is complete and unchanging while .2 and .3 are implemented.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:19Z"},{"id":77,"issue_id":"bd-gsjqf.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FRESH-EYES: MISSING DOCUMENTATION OCCURRENCES.

The audit lists only 6 expected findings (all code invocations). Prior analysis found 9 MORE in documentation/comments/help-text that must also be tracked:

7. scripts/lib/update.sh:1232 — local cmd_display="claude update" (string literal — update display string)
8. scripts/lib/update.sh:2151 — "agents:   Claude Code (claude update)" (help text)
9. scripts/lib/update.sh:2187 — "claude update" (troubleshooting text)
10. acfs.manifest.yaml:652 — documentation note referencing "built-in claude update command"
11. README.md:620 — update table showing "claude update"
12. README.md:1242 — update instruction text
13. acfs/onboard/lessons/08_keeping_updated.md:131 — troubleshooting section
14. apps/web/components/lessons/keeping-updated-lesson.tsx:218 — web lesson component
15. PLAN_TO_CREATE_ACFS.md:190 — old alias definition (leave as historical)

Classification:
- Items 1-5: Code invocations → fix in bd-gsjqf.2
- Item 6: Alias → fix in bd-gsjqf.3
- Item 7: String literal → fix in bd-gsjqf.2 (same file)
- Items 8-9: Help text → fix in bd-gsjqf.2 (same file) or bd-gsjqf.3
- Items 10-14: Documentation → fix in bd-gsjqf.3
- Item 15: Planning doc → leave as historical record

The full 15-item list must be the scope document that bd-gsjqf.2 and bd-gsjqf.3 consume.
